ORDER GRANTING CROSS-DEFENDANT ALLISON LESHEFSKY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS (CCP SECTION 425.16(C)(1)) (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT ALLISON LESHEFSKY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS [C.C.P. 425.16(C)(1)]IS GRANTED IN PART. MS. LESHEFSKY IS AWARDED $33,033.05 IN FEES AND COSTS AGAINST CROSS-COMPLAINANT ANNE KIHAGI SWAIN. BECAUSE SHE PREVAILED ON HER ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AS TO ALL ALLEGATIONS ARISING FROM PROTECTED CONDUCT AND SUCCESSFULLY OBTAINED AN ORDER STRIKING THE GREAT MAJORITY OF THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, MS. LESHEFSKY IS ENTITLED TO FEES FOR ALL TIME HER COUNSEL REASONABLY SPENT ON HER ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND THIS MOTION FOR FEES AND ALL COSTS REASONABLY INCURRED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. THE COURT CREDITS MR. COLLIER'S DECLARATIONS AS TO THE TIME HE SPENT ON THE MOTIONS AND COSTS INCURRED ON THE MOTIONS. HOWEVER, WITHOUT IMPLYING ANY CRITICISM OF MR. COLLIER, THE COURT BELIEVES THAT HIS REQUESTED HOURLY RATE OF $650 IS OUT OF LINE FOR THE WORK HE PERFORMED AND THE TIME HE TOOK TO DO THE WORK. MUCH OF THE WORK HE DID WOULD HAVE BEEN DONE BY EITHER A JUNIOR ASSOCIATE OR A PARALEGAL OR LEGAL ASSISTANT AT A MUCH LOWER RATE IF MS. LESHEFSKY HAD RETAINED A PRIVATE LAW FIRM. Ms. Leshefsky is awarded $33,033.05 in fees and costs against cross-complainant Anne Kihagi Swain. Because she prevailed on her anti-SLAPP motion as to all allegations arising from protected conduct and successfully obtained an order striking the great majority of the allegations in the cross-complaint, Ms. Leshefsky is entitled to fees for all time her counsel reasonably spent on her anti-SLAPP motion and this motion for fees and all costs reasonably incurred for both motions. The court credits Mr. Collier's declarations as to the time he spent on the motions and costs incurred on the motions. However, without implying any criticism of Mr. Collier, the court believes that his requested hourly rate of $650 is out of line for the work he performed and the time he took to do the work. Much of the work he did would have been done by either a junior associate or a paralegal or legal assistant at a much lower rate if Ms. Leshefsky had retained a private law firm. An hourly rate of $400 takes into account that Mr. Collier did all of the work on both motions himself. All of the arguments (particularly those seeking to re-litigate the anti-SLAPP motion) made by Ms. Swain why the motion should be denied or Ms. Leshefsky awarded a lesser amount lack merit. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendant Renka Prop, Llc And Anne Swain'S Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Served On Century Electric, Fog City Fire, Joseph S. Skyes, And Lev Yurovsky
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen L. Collier, of Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Tel. 415.771.9850, for plaintiffs;
No appearance by defendants.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Renka Prop, LLC And Anne Swain's Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Served On Century Electric, Fog City Fire, Joseph S. Skyes, And Lev Yurovsky is denied. The business records have already been produced and the motion is moot. Further, defendant has waived objections to the production of these business records by failing to timely object to production of the documents during the depositions of the nonparties. (Unzipped Apparel, LLC v. Bader (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 123, 132; See, also, Naser v. Lakeridge Athletic Club (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 571, 577-578 (obtaining business records from a third party via deposition subpoena is a deposition under the Discovery Act).). Plaintiff's request for sanctions in the amount of $900. is granted. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
